does_not_support: a new QPE, BBHT, or iterative amplitude-estimation theorem
asymptotic quantum advantage or a matching lower bound
hardware feasibility, noise robustness, or simulator speedup
an application-domain performance or advantage claim
a generic-composition separation for the orientation candidate
a proved unknown-boundary activity-history transducer theorem
supports: finite-QPE resolution and verifier calibration diagnostics
small exact-state BBHT behavior on randomized angular instances
negative controls, failure semantics, and reproducibility
analytic composition falsification of the declared orientation proxy
analytic no-free-QRAM audit for the new unknown-boundary candidate

provenance.reproduction_scope: scientific records should match for the same source, configuration, runtime, and seeds after excluding declared volatile fields

notes: All direct quantum records execute the exact NumPy statevector backend and charged oracle interface.

Instance truth is evaluation-only; direct Top-k receives no explicit membership tuple, but post-QPE validation uses the full boundary intervals and therefore carries membership-equivalent information.
Analytic iterative AE is a separate measurement-law backend and is never mixed with exact-state gate or qubit resources.
Boundary-only is a negative control because its calibration certificate already contains complete membership.
The index-only diffusion is explicitly invalid and never produces an algorithm answer.
Finite-size slopes and simulator results are diagnostics, not asymptotic proofs or hardware speedup evidence.
The n=3m orientation family fails its novelty gate because coarse grouping plus strong-oracle BAI matches its candidate leading scale.
The unknown-boundary history suite starts the new no-free-QRAM candidate core; open gates are research obligations, not theorem claims.
